section_name: Operation of Vehicle with Insufficient Undercarriage Clearance
text:
(a) An operator may not drive on or cross a railroad grade crossing unless the vehicle being operated has sufficient undercarriage clearance.

(b) An offense under this section is a misdemeanor punishable by a fine of not less than $50 or more than $200.
markdown:
# Transp. Code
## Title 7
### Chapter 545. Operation & Movement of Vehicles
##### § 545.427 — Operation of Vehicle with Insufficient Undercarriage Clearance

(a) An operator may not drive on or cross a railroad grade crossing unless the vehicle being operated has sufficient undercarriage clearance.

(b) An offense under this section is a misdemeanor punishable by a fine of not less than $50 or more than $200.

*Renumbered from Transportation Code, Section545.426 (Operation of School Bus)by Acts 2009, 81st Leg., R.S., Ch. 87 (S.B.1969), Sec. 27.001(108), eff. September 1, 2009.*

subsections.label: a
subsections.text: An operator may not drive on or cross a railroad grade crossing unless the vehicle being operated has sufficient undercarriage clearance.

subsections.label: b
subsections.text: An offense under this section is a misdemeanor punishable by a fine of not less than $50 or more than $200.
